explanations.1: @echo off turns off the display of each command before it runs, so you only see the output, not the commands themselves.
explanations.5: This checks if the user provided any command-line argument; if not, it jumps to the help section.
explanations.12: This is the app name converted to hexadecimal characters so it can be used in a special VSCode remote URL format.
explanations.18: The quotes around start are special syntax in batch—they're not part of the command name, they just help batch parse the arguments correctly.
explanations.47: This constructs a special VSCode URL using the hex-encoded app name so VSCode knows which Docker container to attach to.
explanations.65: The %* symbol captures all remaining arguments passed to this function as one string.
explanations.66: This removes the first and last character (the surrounding quotes) from the error message so it displays cleanly.
explanations.68: The [31m and [0m are ANSI color codes—they make the error message display in red in the terminal.
